tickets.title: US001: Manage Safe Exchange Zones
tickets.description:
As a Campus Administrator, I want to manage and update the list of designated "Safe Exchange Zones", so that students always have access to secure, pre-approved meetup locations.
Acceptance Criteria:
Pass: The administrator can add, edit, or remove locations from the authorized Safe Exchange Zones management panel.
Pass: Updated locations immediately reflect in the selection options available to students during the chat initialization process.

tickets.title: US002: Exclusive .edu Domain Registration
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to create an account using my valid university ".edu" email, so that I can access an exclusive campus-only marketplace.
Acceptance Criteria:
Pass: The system allows registration only if the provided email domain ends strictly with ".edu".
Pass: The system rejects non-.edu emails and displays an error message blocking the account creation.

tickets.title: US003: Mandatory Safe Zone Selection for Chat
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want the app to force the selection of a safe exchange zone before I can chat with a buyer/seller, so that my physical meetup remains secure.
Acceptance Criteria:
Pass: The chat interface between a buyer and seller remains completely locked until a "Safe Exchange Zone" is selected from the pre-approved list.
Pass: The selected meeting location is clearly pinned and visible at the top of the chat window once unlocked.

tickets.title: US004: Textbook Barcode Scanning
tickets.description:
As a Student selling textbooks, I want to scan the barcode with my phone camera, so that I can automatically populate the item details without manual typing.
Acceptance Criteria:
Pass: The app successfully requests permission and opens the smartphone camera to scan a standard ISBN barcode.
Pass: The system correctly parses the scanned barcode and automatically populates the textbook's Title and Author fields using an integrated database.

tickets.title: US005: Item Auction System
tickets.description:
As a Student selling items, I want to list my items for auction, so that I can sell to the highest bidder instead of a fixed price.
Acceptance Criteria:
Pass: The seller can select "Auction" as a listing type, then input a starting price and an auction expiration duration.
Pass: Other verified students can place incremental bids, and the system blocks any bid that is lower than or equal to the current highest bid.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 1: The system must restrict account creation and login access exclusively
tickets.description: The system must restrict account creation and login access exclusively to users holding a valid ".edu" university email address.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 2: The application must provide an intuitive mobile interface that allows
tickets.description: The application must provide an intuitive mobile interface that allows users to quickly scan item barcodes using their smartphone cameras to minimize manual data entry.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 3: The system must enforce safety protocols by requiring users to explici
tickets.description: The system must enforce safety protocols by requiring users to explicitly agree upon a designated "Safe Exchange Zone" before peer-to-peer communication features are unlocked.

analysis.ambiguity1: "Can I just scan the barcode with my phone camera?" The requirement mentions scanning a barcode but lacks technical details on the subsequent system behavior. It does not specify whether the app needs to integrate with an external database (e.g., Google Books API) to automatically fetch and populate the textbook's title and author, or how the system should handle unrecognized barcodes.
analysis.ambiguity2: "The app should force users to select a safe zone before they can chat." It is unclear how "safe zones" are populated and managed within the system. Does the app provide a hardcoded dropdown list of pre-approved locations managed by an administrator, or does it use location services/maps? Additionally, it is vague whether selecting the zone forms a binding meeting agreement or merely a suggestion to initialize the chat.
analysis.conflictSolution: The University Legal Team explicitly states the app cannot process payments, but the Student Union President wants the app to collect a 2% transaction fee from sales. The system cannot automatically extract a percentage if it does not act as the payment processor. Adhere strictly to the Legal Team's constraint by not processing direct peer-to-peer payments. To satisfy the Student Union's funding goal, pivot from a per-transaction fee to a flat "premium listing fee" or a small per-semester subscription model powered by an approved external third-party vendor (like Stripe) that operates completely outside the university's financial liability.
analysis.nfr1: The system must restrict account creation and login access exclusively to users holding a valid ".edu" university email address.
analysis.nfr2: The application must provide an intuitive mobile interface that allows users to quickly scan item barcodes using their smartphone cameras to minimize manual data entry.
analysis.nfr3: The system must enforce safety protocols by requiring users to explicitly agree upon a designated "Safe Exchange Zone" before peer-to-peer communication features are unlocked.
